title: Give the workspace a per-window VSCode identity
description: Write .vscode/settings.json so this project's window is visually distinguishable from other open windows: an emoji in window.title (dock / Alt-Tab / window switcher) and a quiet design-system-coloured band on the chosen bar. Deterministic and idempotent: colours are DERIVED (a design-system primary, or a stable hash of the project name when there's no design system yet), the file is MERGED not clobbered (the project's own VSCode settings survive), and a provenance marker (.vscode/.window-identity.json) drives a no-clobber ratchet so a --repair never downgrades a design-system or hand-picked colour back to the name-hash placeholder.

name.description: The name that seeds the name-hash placeholder colour. Defaults to the workspace package.json name (scope stripped). The window.title label itself uses VSCode's ${rootName}, so it tracks the folder regardless.

primary.description: The design-system primary as a hex colour (e.g. #D97757). When given, the band is derived from this and the default source becomes 'design-system'; when omitted, the band is derived from the name hash. Supplied by the window-identity skill once the project has a design system.

emoji.description: The window emoji, prepended to window.title. Defaults to the existing marker's emoji, then to a neutral placeholder. The colour already makes each window distinct, so a generic emoji at baseline is acceptable; the skill supplies a project-fitting one.

surface.description: Which window surfaces to drive. 'title' = emoji only. 'status' = emoji + status bar (default; always rendered, no custom-title-bar dependency). 'titlebar' = emoji + title bar (needs window.titleBarStyle: custom). 'both' = emoji + both bars.

personal.description: Git-ignore .vscode/settings.json and the provenance marker so the identity is this developer's own rather than committed team-wide. Note: this ignores the WHOLE settings.json, not just the identity keys.

source.description: Provenance driving the no-clobber ratchet (name-hash < design-system < manual). Defaults to 'design-system' when --primary is passed, else 'name-hash'. Pass 'manual' when a human deliberately picks the colour/emoji so no automated run ever overwrites it.
